sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Oil executives rush to complete contract migration in Venezuela, sources say
feed.summary: Venezuela's oil ministry is pressing foreign and local companies partnered with state energy firm PDVSA to finalize new oil and gas contracts by July 28, following a legal framework reform in January. The new framework offers autonomy and a revised taxation model, with a weighted royalty rate being a key negotiation point. While existing partners, including Chevron, are rushing to meet the deadline, new entrants will have more time to complete their agreements.

feed.title: Brown & Brown selects Anthropic, McKinsey and Accenture to support AI transformation
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. has partnered with Anthropic, McKinsey & Company, and Accenture to establish artificial intelligence (AI) as a foundational enterprise capability. This initiative aims to responsibly integrate AI across the organization to enhance customer experiences, improve productivity, and accelerate growth by transforming key business processes. The program includes deploying Anthropic’s Claude across 23,000 teammates and establishing a value management office to monitor AI initiatives and ensure disciplined execution.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern (NSC) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NSC) reported record Q2 2026 revenues of $3.5 billion, an 11% year-over-year increase, with adjusted net income rising 7% to $793M and adjusted EPS at $3.52. This growth was fueled by strong volume in energy, intermodal, and industrial markets, along with higher fuel surcharges. Despite a 15% increase in operational costs due to fuel expenses, adjusted operating income grew by 5% to $1.2 billion, and the company updated its 2026 operating expense guidance to reflect incremental fuel costs.

feed.title: HP Brings Enterprise-Grade Android to Retail and Hospitality
feed.summary: HP has introduced the HP Engage One Pro G2q, an enterprise-grade Android all-in-one point-of-sale platform designed for retail and hospitality sectors. This new offering runs Android 16 with Google Mobile Services and is powered by the Qualcomm Dragonwing® Q-6690 processor, providing a robust solution for modernization. HP's strategy aims to offer customers OS choice, including Windows, Linux, and Android, ensuring consistent operational models and addressing critical needs like security, manageability, and peripheral integration.

feed.title: GE and Magellan agree on Canadian F414 MRO for Gripen programme
feed.summary: GE Aerospace and Magellan Aerospace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) for F414-GE-39E engine sustainment, crucial for the Saab JAS 39 Gripen E fighter. This agreement is contingent on Canada selecting the Gripen E for its future Royal Canadian Air Force fleet. The MoU would establish Magellan's Mississauga facility as a Canadian center of excellence for F414 engine maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO).

feed.title: Old Dominion Freight Line Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.29 per Share, Payable Sept. 16 to Shareholders of Record on Sept. 2
feed.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.29 per share, maintaining its previous payout. This dividend is scheduled to be paid on September 16, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of September 2, 2026. This announcement indicates a consistent return to its investors.

feed.title: Defending Champions Ready to Take on Stacked Field at 48th Bank of America Chicago Marathon
feed.summary: The 48th Bank of America Chicago Marathon on October 11, 2026, will feature reigning champions Jacob Kiplimo and Hawi Feysa defending their titles against a strong international field, including Brigid Kosgei and Charles Hicks. More than 55,000 runners from over 100 countries are expected, contributing an estimated $755.9 million in economic impact to Chicago. In the wheelchair division, Marcel Hug aims for his fifth consecutive win, while nine-time champion Tatyana McFadden returns.

feed.title: United Bancorp Q2 Earnings: EPS Rises 9.1% to $0.36 | UBCP Stock News
feed.summary: United Bancorp (NASDAQ: UBCP) reported strong second quarter and first-half 2026 earnings, with Q2 diluted EPS rising 9.1% to $0.36 and net income increasing 9.4% to $2.095 million. For the first six months, diluted EPS was up 6.2% to $0.69 and net income grew 5.8% to $4.006 million. The company experienced significant deposit growth of $44 million (6.8%) in the first half, reaching $686.9 million, with a substantial portion in lower-cost accounts, and maintained a healthy capital position despite an increase in nonaccrual loans primarily due to one commercial relationship.

feed.title: Which AI chatbots do patients turn to for medical advice?
feed.summary: A new Pollfish survey reveals that when seeking medical advice, particularly about health insurance, patients primarily use ChatGPT, followed by Google's Gemini. Many respondents leverage AI to understand benefits, medical bills, and as an alternative when they cannot afford doctor visits. Despite AI's growing popularity for health information, over a third of patients prefer not to use it, emphasizing the ongoing need for traditional communication channels in healthcare.

feed.title: FDA recalls baby food pouches after possible plastic contamination
feed.summary: PT Organics Limited has voluntarily recalled specific Pumpkin Tree Peter Rabbit Organics Banana & Strawberry fruit puree pouches due to a packaging defect that could lead to soft, food-grade plastic strands being present in the product. The recalled pouches were sold nationwide at Target, Kroger, and Meijer stores between March 6 and July 13, 2026, with no injuries reported to date. Consumers are advised to check product barcodes and best-by dates, and to dispose of or return recalled pouches for a refund.

feed.title: General Mills offers the magic of Harry Potter at home with Betty Crocker and Pillsbury treats
feed.summary: General Mills has announced a new and returning lineup of Harry Potter-inspired products from Pillsbury and Betty Crocker, available at retailers nationwide. These treats, including Golden Snitch cookies and Chocolate Frog truffle kits, celebrate the Harry Potter universe ahead of a new television adaptation launching on Christmas Day 2026

feed.title: MSFT DEADLINE: SueWallSt Reminds Microsoft Corporation Investors of Upcoming Securities Class Action Deadline
feed.summary: SueWallSt is reminding Microsoft Corporation (NASDAQ: MSFT) investors of an upcoming August 11, 2026 deadline to file as lead plaintiff in a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Microsoft made misleading statements about its AI initiatives, including Copilot and Azure, while concealing significant technical problems, leading to investor losses. Investors who purchased MSFT securities between May 1, 2025, and January 28, 2026, and wish to be considered lead plaintiff, should contact SueWallSt.

feed.title: SueWallSt Reminds Shareholders of a Lead Plaintiff Deadline of September 14, 2026 in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. Lawsuit - REGN
feed.summary: SueWallSt is alerting investors in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (REGN) about a securities class action lawsuit with a lead plaintiff deadline of September 14, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Regeneron minimized risks associated with its Phase III Fianlimab-Libtayo study, leading to a significant stock price decline after disclosures revealed the trial did not meet its primary endpoint. Shareholders who purchased REGN securities between August 1, 2025, and May 15, 2026, and suffered losses may be eligible to participate.

feed.title: T-Mobile reports quarterly earnings beat, raises free cash flow outlook
feed.summary: T-Mobile (NASDAQ:TMUS) reported second-quarter earnings that surpassed analyst expectations, driven by higher-than-anticipated adjusted earnings per share and service revenue growth. Despite a slight miss on revenue estimates and a sequential slowdown in postpaid account additions, the company raised its full-year adjusted free cash flow outlook. Shares modestly declined as investors weighed the revenue shortfall against the earnings beat and increased free cash flow forecast.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line stock may move 7.3% on earnings release
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) stock is projected to move 7.3% following its upcoming earnings report on July 30, according to options data. Historical analysis reveals that in five of the last eight earnings announcements, the actual stock movement exceeded the implied change from options trading. For example, in November 2025, the stock fell 20.1% against an implied 6.5% move, highlighting the potential for significant post-earnings volatility.

feed.title: Erie Indemnity Co (ERIE) CEO gains 87.541 deferred share credits
feed.summary: Erie Indemnity Company's CEO, Timothy G. Necastro, received 87.541 Incentive Compensation Deferral Plan Share Credits on July 21, 2026, through dividend reinvestment. These credits represent a right to an equivalent number of Class A common shares upon his retirement or separation. Following this transaction, Mr. Necastro holds a total of 13005.8790 Share Credits, along with direct and indirect holdings of Class A common stock.

feed.title: Why China Could Become Costco's Next Membership Catalyst
feed.summary: Costco's Executive Membership program in China is showing strong early adoption, indicating significant growth potential for the company's membership income and loyalty. The company's management highlights China, along with Japan and Korea, as key international regions for future warehouse development. Despite a recent dip in share price, Costco's valuation remains premium compared to competitors, with positive sales and earnings per share growth projected for the coming years.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended June 30, 2026
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated has released its earnings for the second quarter and six months ending June 30, 2026. For the second quarter, net interest income increased to $2,052 million from $1,467 million a year ago, with net income rising to $727 million from $536 million. Diluted earnings per share for the quarter were $0.33, compared to $0.34 in the prior year.

feed.title: Did General Dynamics' (GD) General Counsel Transition Just Recast Its Leadership Stability Story?
feed.summary: General Dynamics announced the retirement of its long-time general counsel, Greg Gallopoulos, and the appointment of Nick Barnaby to the role, effective January 1, 2027. This transition comes amidst strong defense contract momentum and an anticipated earnings report. While the leadership change is noted, the article suggests it's immaterial to near-term financial drivers, with the upcoming earnings report and continued execution on major programs being more significant for investors.

feed.title: Liberty Energy (LBRT) Reports Mixed Results And SLB Alliance, Is The Discount Still Real?
feed.summary: Liberty Energy (LBRT) recently reported mixed second-quarter results, showing higher sales but lower earnings, along with a new alliance with SLB focused on data center power needs. Despite a recent 22% stock slide over three months, the stock has a strong 90% one-year return and is considered undervalued by analysts, with a fair value estimate of $33.92 against its current price of $25.14. The company's strategic move into data center power projects is a key factor in its valuation, though future growth depends on successful project scaling and market conditions.

feed.title: Cardinal Health (CAH) Is Buying Strive Medical And AdaptHealth Diabetes Business
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (CAH) is expanding its at-Home Solutions division by acquiring Strive Medical, a provider of urology and wound care supplies, and AdaptHealth's diabetes business. These acquisitions aim to deepen the company's reach in chronic care management for diabetes and urology at home. The deals are expected to be accretive to non-GAAP earnings within a year, enhancing Cardinal Health's position in the growing home-based care market.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares Reports Q2 2026 Results: Full Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ: HBAN) reported a strong Q2 2026, showcasing significant organic growth, expanding revenue, and robust profitability. The successful completion of the Cadence systems conversion has positioned the company for accelerated growth, particularly in high-growth regions like Texas and the South. Management expressed confidence in achieving its 2027 ROTCE target of 18-19%, driven by continued organic growth, expense discipline, and strategic partnerships.

feed.title: Full Transcript: Blackstone Q2 2026 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Blackstone reported strong Q2 2026 earnings, with distributable earnings up 26% year-over-year to $2 billion, driven by significant investments in AI-related infrastructure like data centers and energy. The company's assets under management reached a record $1.35 trillion, with strong inflows across institutional, insurance, and private wealth channels. Blackstone emphasized its leadership in private capital for AI advancements, highlighting strategic partnerships and the launch of new investment vehicles focused on this sector, while also addressing the potential risks and societal implications of AI growth.

feed.title: 1.6 million eggs recalled at Kroger and other stores over salmonella risk, FDA says
feed.summary: Midwest Poultry Services has recalled 1.6 million eggs due to potential salmonella contamination, affecting products sold at Kroger and other retailers in six states. The recalled eggs were produced at Texas farms between June 6 and July 3, with sell-by dates ranging from July 20 to August 17. Consumers are advised not to eat the eggs and to return them for a refund, although no illnesses have been reported.

feed.title: How Talos Energy’s Q1 EPS and EBITDA Beat Amid Revenue Dip At Talos Energy (TALO) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Talos Energy reported a strong first-quarter result, surpassing analyst estimates for EPS and EBITDA despite a slight revenue dip. This performance, coupled with a renewed focus on capital discipline and long-term execution, reinforces its investment narrative as an offshore oil and gas producer prioritizing operational efficiency. The company's expanded equity buyback authorization also signals a commitment to returning excess cash and aligning with its 2026 efficiency plan.

feed.title: Erie Indemnity (ERIE) EVP Sean Dugan acquires additional deferred share credits
feed.summary: Erie Indemnity Company's EVP, Sean Dugan, acquired an additional 8.794 Incentive Compensation Deferral Plan Share Credits on July 21, 2026, through dividend reinvestment. These credits, valued at $215.82 each, represent rights to Class A common shares upon his retirement or separation. Following this transaction, Dugan now holds 1,306.527 Share Credits and 278.650 direct shares of Class A common stock.

feed.title: Prudential Financial stock hits 52-week low at 21.75 USD
feed.summary: Prudential Financial Inc. stock has dropped to a 52-week low of $21.75, representing a significant decline of 4.2% over the last year and nearly 8% in the past six months. The company, valued at $41.28 billion, trades at a P/E ratio of 11.95, with earnings due in six days. The downturn is attributed to a challenging market environment, and investors are closely monitoring the situation.

feed.title: Frisco-based Public Storage Completes Acquisition of National Storage Affiliates
feed.summary: Frisco-based Public Storage has finalized its acquisition of National Storage Affiliates (NSA) in a deal valued at approximately $10.5 billion. This transaction significantly expands Public Storage's portfolio to over 4,500 properties and 327 million rentable square feet, solidifying its position as the world's largest self-storage platform. The acquisition is a key step in Public Storage's PS4.0 strategic plan, aimed at driving shareholder value, improving customer experience, and capturing external growth opportunities, with expected accretion to FFO per share and significant synergies.

feed.title: PepsiCo to cease warehouse operations at Oklahoma production site
feed.summary: PepsiCo plans to close warehouse operations at its Tulsa, Oklahoma, production facility by November 15, 2026, impacting 184 warehouse employees. While production will continue at the existing site, the warehouse duties will be moved to a new facility in the Tulsa area. PepsiCo is offering assistance to affected employees, including help applying for jobs with the new logistics provider, continued pay and benefits, and career support.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Highlights Strong Travel Numbers as Fuel for American Express
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted American Express (AXP) as a strong investment choice, citing its unique cardholder base, high-margin annual fees, and strong travel numbers. Technical analysis from Bob Lang suggests a path to $350-$

feed.title: Tesla, Google among market cap stock movers on Thursday
feed.summary: Thursday's market saw significant stock movements, with Tesla and Google experiencing declines, while Medpace and United Rentals saw substantial gains. The article details the biggest stock movers across mega-cap, large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap categories, highlighting specific company performances and reasons behind the shifts.

feed.title: Edge's Arris Trim Uses Mineral Composite to Limit Joint Gaps
feed.summary: Edge, a brand of UFP Retail Solutions under UFP Industries (NASDAQ: UFPI), has launched Arris™, a new mineral-based composite exterior trim. This product is designed as a low-maintenance, durable, and dimensionally stable alternative to PVC trim, aiming to minimize thermal movement and maintain tight joints. Arris will be showcased at the Southeast Building Conference later this month, highlighting its innovative Surestone® technology and improved performance features.

feed.title: These Analysts Slash Their Forecasts On Las Vegas Sands After Downbeat Q2 Results
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (NYSE: LVS) announced weaker-than-expected second-quarter results, missing consensus estimates for both earnings per share and revenue. Following these results, several analysts revised their price targets for the stock downwards. Despite the downbeat report, Las Vegas Sands shares saw a slight increase on Thursday.

feed.title: California American Water Says No Service Cuts Are Expected
feed.summary: California American Water is investing $2.5 million in its Northern District Well Rehabilitation Program, a multi-year initiative running through December 2027, to upgrade over 100 groundwater wells and maintain water system reliability. This program, which includes rehabilitation work on the Auberry Well in Sacramento, is designed to enhance groundwater production capacity and extend infrastructure life. The company confirmed that no service interruptions are expected for customers during these upgrade activities.

feed.title: Tesla is 'all about' future promises: Why this strategist calls it a 'story' stock
authors: Yahoo Finance Video and Brian Sozzi
feed.summary: Tesla reported negative free cash flows in Q2, primarily due to significant investments in its Optimus robotics ventures, causing its stock to fall by as much as 12%. Brent Schutte, CIO of Northwestern Mutual Wealth Management Company, describes Tesla as a "story" stock, emphasizing that its valuation relies heavily on unproven future promises rather than current earnings. Schutte suggests that investors should focus on companies with actual profits and revenues, especially during periods of increasing interest rates and uncertainty.

feed.title: Digital Realty Trust earnings in focus after $3.5B acquisition By Investing.com
feed.summary: Digital Realty Trust is set to report its second-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating $0.47 per share on $1.66 billion in revenue. The report follows significant strategic moves, including a $3.5 billion acquisition of data centers from Blackstone and a substantial equity offering, aimed at reshaping the REIT's growth trajectory amid increasing AI-driven data center demand. Investors will be focused on management's ability to articulate a clear path to earnings accretion from these investments, the timeline for the Blackstone deal's impact, leasing momentum, and the company's power strategy to address energy infrastructure bottlenecks.

feed.title: Boeing narrowly tops Airbus in muted Farnborough Airshow order race (BA:NYSE)
feed.summary: Boeing Co. (BA) surpassed Airbus (EADSF)(EADSY) in aircraft orders at the Farnborough Airshow, though the event highlighted the industry's shift from large sales announcements to a focus on increasing production. Strong demand is maintaining massive order backlogs for both companies, which are now prioritizing overcoming supply chain issues, labor shortages, and engine production bottlenecks.

feed.title: HCA Healthcare earnings in focus as payer mix pressures mount
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare is poised to report second-quarter earnings, facing scrutiny despite beating revenue and earnings expectations due to concerns over worsening insurance coverage and patient volumes. The hospital operator pre-announced results, revealing a significant "payer mix shift" due to rising uninsured patients and subsequently lowered its full-year profit guidance. Investors will be

feed.title: Raymond James Q3 Earnings Beat on Higher Revenues, Provision Benefit
feed.summary: Raymond James (RJF) reported strong third-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings, beating analyst estimates, driven by record revenues and a benefit from credit losses on bank loans. While net revenues increased significantly across most segments, non-interest expenses also rose. The company maintained a strong balance sheet and capital ratios, and continued share repurchases.

feed.title: Arkansas Sues Pfizer, J&J, Others Over Drug Discount Access
feed.summary: Arkansas is suing 13 pharmaceutical companies, including Pfizer, Johnson & Johnson, and Eli Lilly & Co., alleging they imposed unlawful restrictions on healthcare providers seeking drug discounts under the federal 340B Drug Pricing Program. Attorney General Tim Griffin claims these companies, along with their data management firm, violated the state's deceptive trade practices rules and a law preventing such discount restrictions. The lawsuit targets significant players in the pharmaceutical industry over access to crucial drug pricing benefits.

feed.title: Cummins updates 2027 on-highway truck engine launch plans
feed.summary: Cummins announced updates to its Model Year 2027 North American on-highway truck engine launch plans, including a phased ramp-up of its X10 and X15 engines. This strategy is a response to the EPA's proposed emissions regulations and aims to ensure regulatory compliance, support customer production, and build confidence in new products. The existing X15, X12, and L9 engines will remain available during the transition, with the next-generation B platform launching in January 2028.

feed.title: Breakthrough Properties and Tishman Speyer Provide $90M Mezz Loan for Aperture Del Mar in San Diego
feed.summary: Breakthrough Properties and Tishman Speyer have jointly invested in a $90 million mezzanine bridge loan for Aperture Del Mar, a 538,000-square-foot life science campus in San Diego. The campus is fully leased to Neurocrine Biosciences, serving as its global headquarters. This investment, part of Breakthrough's second life science fund and Tishman Speyer's new debt platform, helps refinance an existing loan for the borrower, Gemdale USA.

feed.title: Erie Indemnity (ERIE) EVP acquires 8.699 deferred share credits via plan
feed.summary: Erie Indemnity Executive Vice President Cody Cook acquired 8.699 Incentive Compensation Deferral Plan Share Credits on July 21, 2026, through dividend reinvestment. These credits entitle him to an equivalent number of Class A common shares upon retirement or separation from the

feed.title: State Street (STT) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: State Street reported record Q2 2026 earnings, with total revenue up 17% year-over-year to $4 billion and EPS of $3.65. The company announced new medium-term targets including a 35% pretax margin and mid-20s ROTCE, driven by a $1 billion transformation program focused on technology, AI, and strategic growth initiatives in alternatives, digital assets, and wealth services. They also increased their quarterly common stock dividend by 10% to $0.92 per share following strong financial performance and a robust capital position.

feed.title: HST Maintains by Wells Fargo -- Price Target Raised to $26.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analyst Cooper Clark maintained the price target for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) but raised it to $26.00, marking a 4% increase, while keeping an Overweight rating. Despite this positive analyst outlook, GuruFocus indicates HST is 23.1% overvalued at its current price of $24.44 compared to its GF Value™ of $19.86. The company has a strong GF Score™ of 81/100, driven by profitability and momentum, but insider activity shows $1.7 million in shares sold over the last three months with no purchases.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin, Quest Diagnostics, Thermo Fisher Scientific And Other Big Stocks Moving Higher On Thursd
feed.summary: Several large-cap stocks, including Lockheed Martin, Quest Diagnostics, and Thermo Fisher Scientific, are experiencing significant gains on Thursday after reporting better-than-expected quarterly financial results and, in many cases, raising their full-year guidance. Lockheed Martin reported strong earnings and sales, leading to an 11.4% jump in its shares. Other companies like NVE Corp, Novocure, Cleveland-Cliffs, Medpace Holdings, United Rentals, Oceaneering International, Kaiser Aluminum Corp, Roper Technologies, RTX Corp, Norfolk Southern Corp, and Honeywell International also saw substantial share price increases following positive financial reports.

feed.title: Tenet Healthcare faces earnings test amid hospital sector headwinds By Investing.com
feed.summary: Tenet Healthcare is preparing to report second-quarter earnings, with analysts projecting growth in earnings and revenue despite sector-wide headwinds, particularly in hospital utilization. While most analysts rate the stock a "Strong Buy," concerns persist about physician staffing, outpatient pricing trends, and the stability of hospital margins. Investors will closely watch whether Tenet's diversified model can sustain growth amidst these challenges.

feed.title: Dow Says Iran War Could Boost Guidance as Supply Chain Shielded
feed.summary: Dow Inc. anticipates that ongoing Middle East tensions could increase plastics and packaging prices, benefiting its profits without raising material costs due to abundant U.S. natural gas and ethane supplies. This scenario could lead to an upside in the company's third-quarter guidance, as stated by CEO Karen Carter. The company's supply chain is shielded, allowing it to capitalize on rising oil prices.

feed.title: PGR (NYSE: PGR) insider schedules Rule 144 sale after stock vesting
feed.summary: An insider at Progressive Corp (PGR), Steven Broz, has filed a Form 144 notice to sell 1,156 common shares valued at $237,176.52. The shares originate from a restricted stock vesting on January 20, 2026, and the sale is scheduled for around July 23, 2026, on the NYSE. The filing also discloses prior sales by Broz of 1,157 shares on June 5, 2026, and another 1,157 shares on June 22, 2026.

feed.title: Fastenal Company (FAST) Gains from Reshoring Trends and AI Infrastructure Buildout
feed.summary: Fastenal Company (FAST) is highlighted by Brasada Capital Management in its Q2 2026 investor letter as a high-quality business benefiting from reshoring trends and AI infrastructure buildout. Fastenal provides essential industrial and construction supplies and inventory management solutions, embedding itself within client operations to ensure seamless supply chains. The company is valued for its reliable service, pricing power, and decentralized business model, though it is not among the top AI stock picks for its hedge fund.
